subject: Stanford Online Learning Opportunities
body:
[1][jpeg]

[2]Stanford Online

LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES

[3]Stanford University
______________________________________________________________________

UPCOMING COURSES
______________________________________________________________________

[4]ALGORITHMS
[5]ALGORITHMS, DESIGN AND ANALYSIS PART I
Starts January 19
Learn fundamental principles of algorithm design: divide-and-conquer
methods, graph algorithms, practical data structures (heaps, hash tables,
search trees), randomized algorithms, and more. (Free.)
[6]learn more >
[7]CRYPTO I
[8]CRYPTOGRAPHY I
Starts January 5
Protect information in computer systems by understanding the security of
cryptographic constructions and applying this knowledge in the real world.
(Free.)
[9]learn more >
[10]DIGGING DEEPER
[11]DIGGING DEEPER: MAKING MANUSCRIPTS
Starts January 20
This course introduces you to the study of early texts, focusing
principally on the medieval book and covering other textual objects, such
as scrolls and diplomata. (Free.)
[12]learn more >
[13]GAME THEORY II
[14]GAME THEORY II: ADVANCED APPLICATIONS
Starts January 11
Learn how to design interactions between agents to achieve good social
outcomes through social choice theory (i.e. collective decision making),
mechanism design, and auctions. (Free.)
[15]learn more >
[16]INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S HEALTH AND HUMAN RIGHTS
[17]INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S HEALTH AND HUMAN RIGHTS
Starts January 29
Take part in interactive discussions and cooperative exercises to learn how
to engage with your communities and deepen understanding of the issues
women face in real-life circumstances. (Free.)
[18]learn more >
[19]QUANTUM MECHANICS
[20]QUANTUM MECHANICS FOR SCIENTISTS AND ENGINEERS 2
Starts January 13
Learn core advanced concepts and the ways in which quantum mechanics is
written and used in modern practice. (Free.)
[21]learn more >
[22]STATISTICAL LEARNING
[23]STATISTICAL LEARNING
Starts January 19
Learn about introductory linear and polynomial regression, logistic
regression, linear discriminant analysis, and more with methods described
without heavy reliance on formulas or complex mathematics. (Free.)
[24]learn more >
[25]TECH ENTREPRENEURSHIP
[26]TECHNOLOGY ENTREPRENEURSHIP PART I
Starts January 5
Discover the processes used to develop startups, and work in teams to gain
experience you can use to create a business model, market an
entrepreneurial idea to potential investors, and manage rapid growth.
(Free.)
[27]learn more >
______________________________________________________________________

SELF-STUDY & SELF-PACED COURSES
______________________________________________________________________

[28]ORGANIZATIONAL ANALYSIS
[29]ORGANIZATIONAL ANALYSIS
NOW OPEN!
Make sense of the challenges organizations face and learn organizational
theories to direct organizational change. (Free.)
[30]learn more >
[31]CHEMICAL ENGINEERING
[32]INTRODUCTION TO CHEMICAL ENGINEERING
NOW OPEN!
Get an overview of chemical engineering through discussions and analysis of
overall staged separations, material and energy balances, concepts of rate
processes, energy and mass transport, and kinetics of chemical reactions.
(Free.)
[33]learn more >
______________________________________________________________________

CONTINUING MEDICAL EDUCATION
______________________________________________________________________

[34]SURGICAL IMPROVEMENT OF CLINICAL KNOWLEDGE OPS
[35]SICKO - SURGICAL IMPROVEMENT OF CLINICAL KNOWLEDGE OPS
NOW OPEN!
If you’re a surgeon, you can hone your surgical decision-making skills in
treating acute and sub-acute surgical conditions and complications through
this game-based, interactive course. (Fee Applies.)
[36]learn more >
[37]TTE BASICS
[38]TTE BASICS
NOW OPEN!
Handle medical emergencies and manage hemodynamically unstable patients in
inpatient and outpatient settings to enhance diagnosis and care through the
use of HCU’s. (Fee Applies.)
[39]learn more >
______________________________________________________________________

STANFORD CENTER FOR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT
______________________________________________________________________

[40]CONVERTING STRATEGY INTO ACTION
[41]CONVERTING STRATEGY INTO ACTION
Now Open!
Improve organizational alignment and achieve your strategic objectives with
a stronger project-based execution that consistently delivers high
performance. This online professional course contributes to the Stanford
Advanced Project Management certificate. (Fee Applies.)
[42]learn more >
[43]DECISION QUALITY
[44]DECISION QUALITY
Now Open!
Make smarter, faster, more creative long-term decisions and learn how to
build decision competency into your organization. This online professional
course contributes to the Strategic Decision and Risk Management
certificate. (Fee Applies.)
[45]learn more >
______________________________________________________________________

STANFORD CONTINUING STUDIES
______________________________________________________________________

[46]ARCHEOLOGY AND ANCIENT ENGINEERING
[47]ARCHEOLOGY AND ANCIENT  ENGINEERING
Starts January 12
Explore many of the world’s most famous ancient engineering projects and
inventions. From Stonehenge to the Egyptian pyramids to the irrigation
systems of Persia, this course enables you to examine these astonishing
achievements from an engineering and technology perspective. (Fee Applies.)
[48]learn more >
[49]CONSTITUTIONAL LAW
[50]CONSTITUTIONAL LAW
Starts January 12
Examine our nation’s founding charter and the seminal Supreme Court cases
interpreting it. Using Supreme Court opinions as a guide, this course will
focus on the Constitution’s basic structure and design, investigating the
principles of judicial review, federalism, and separation of powers. (Fee
Applies.)
[51]learn more >
[52]SCIENCE OF WILLPOWER AND CHANGE
[53]THE SCIENCE OF WILLPOWER AND CHANGE
Starts February 9
This course explains what willpower is, why it matters, and how to develop
it by drawing on psychology, neuroscience, medicine, and mind-body
research. Addressing physical health, emotional well-being, social
relationships, and professional success, this course will help you gain
greater focus and motivation.(Fee Applies.)
[54]learn more >
______________________________________________________________________

OTHER LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES
______________________________________________________________________

[55]INNOVATION AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP
[56]STANFORD INNOVATION AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP CERTIFICATE
Now Open!
Learn essential skills and effective strategies for working in and managing
innovative organizations – and for starting new ones. Certificate requires
8 out of 12 self-paced courses and may be taken in any order. (Application
and Fee Apply.)
[57]learn more >
[58]LEAD CERTIFICATE
[59]STANFORD LEAD CERTIFICATE: CORPORATE INNOVATION
Starts May 5, Application Deadline March 25
Master the key business fundamentals of finance, strategy, and critical
thinking. Deepen your knowledge of methods for innovating through design
thinking, building business models, and overcoming resistance to change.
(Application and Fee Apply.)
[60]learn more >

[61]View our Catalog >

Thank you for your interest in learning opportunities available through
[62]Stanford Online. We offer many free, online courses taught by Stanford
faculty to lifelong learners worldwide for self-enrichment. We also offer a
variety of professional education opportunities in conjunction with many of
the university's schools and departments. Courses available through
Stanford Online are listed at [63]http://online.stanford.edu/courses/.

Additional courses and programs are offered by: [64]Stanford Center for
Professional Development (School of Engineering), [65]Stanford Innovation
and Entrepreneurship (Graduate School of Business and School of
Engineering), [66]Continuing Medical Education (School of Medicine) and
[67]Continuing Studies. (Fees may apply.)

Follow Us:

[68][facebook.gif] [69][twitter.gif]

© Stanford University, Stanford, California 94305
[70]Contact Us

Manage your subscription

LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES

UPCOMING COURSES

 
ALGORITHMS, DESIGN AND ANALYSIS PART I
Starts January 19
Learn fundamental principles of algorithm design: divide-and-conquer methods, graph algorithms, practical data structures (heaps, hash tables, search trees), randomized algorithms, and more. (Free.)
learn more >
CRYPTOGRAPHY I
Starts January 5
Protect information in computer systems by understanding the security of cryptographic constructions and applying this knowledge in the real world. (Free.)
learn more >
DIGGING DEEPER: MAKING MANUSCRIPTS
Starts January 20
This course introduces you to the study of early texts, focusing principally on the medieval book and covering other textual objects, such as scrolls and diplomata. (Free.)
learn more >
GAME THEORY II: ADVANCED APPLICATIONS
Starts January 11
Learn how to design interactions between agents to achieve good social outcomes through social choice theory (i.e. collective decision making), mechanism design, and auctions. (Free.)
learn more >
INTERNATIONAL WOMEN'S HEALTH AND HUMAN RIGHTS
Starts January 29
Take part in interactive discussions and cooperative exercises to learn how to engage with your communities and deepen understanding of the issues women face in real-life circumstances. (Free.)
learn more >
QUANTUM MECHANICS FOR SCIENTISTS AND ENGINEERS 2
Starts January 13
Learn core advanced concepts and the ways in which quantum mechanics is written and used in modern practice. (Free.)
learn more >
STATISTICAL LEARNING
Starts January 19
Learn about introductory linear and polynomial regression, logistic regression, linear discriminant analysis, and more with methods described without heavy reliance on formulas or complex mathematics. (Free.)
learn more >
TECHNOLOGY ENTREPRENEURSHIP PART I
Starts January 5
Discover the processes used to develop startups, and work in teams to gain experience you can use to create a business model, market an entrepreneurial idea to potential investors, and manage rapid growth. (Free.)
learn more >
SELF-STUDY & SELF-PACED COURSES

 
ORGANIZATIONAL ANALYSIS
NOW OPEN!
Make sense of the challenges organizations face and learn organizational theories to direct organizational change. (Free.)
learn more >
INTRODUCTION TO CHEMICAL ENGINEERING
NOW OPEN!
Get an overview of chemical engineering through discussions and analysis of overall staged separations, material and energy balances, concepts of rate processes, energy and mass transport, and kinetics of chemical reactions. (Free.)
learn more >
CONTINUING MEDICAL EDUCATION

 
SICKO - SURGICAL IMPROVEMENT OF CLINICAL KNOWLEDGE OPS
NOW OPEN!
If you’re a surgeon, you can hone your surgical decision-making skills in treating acute and sub-acute surgical conditions and complications through this game-based, interactive course. (Fee Applies.)
learn more >
TTE BASICS
NOW OPEN!
Handle medical emergencies and manage hemodynamically unstable patients in inpatient and outpatient settings to enhance diagnosis and care through the use of HCU’s. (Fee Applies.)
learn more >
STANFORD CENTER FOR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT

 
CONVERTING STRATEGY INTO ACTION
Now Open!
Improve organizational alignment and achieve your strategic objectives with a stronger project-based execution that consistently delivers high performance. This online professional course contributes to the Stanford Advanced Project Management certificate. (Fee Applies.)
learn more >
DECISION QUALITY
Now Open!
Make smarter, faster, more creative long-term decisions and learn how to build decision competency into your organization. This online professional course contributes to the Strategic Decision and Risk Management certificate. (Fee Applies.)
learn more >
STANFORD CONTINUING STUDIES

 
ARCHEOLOGY AND ANCIENT  ENGINEERING
Starts January 12
Explore many of the world’s most famous ancient engineering projects and inventions. From Stonehenge to the Egyptian pyramids to the irrigation systems of Persia, this course enables you to examine these astonishing achievements from an engineering and technology perspective. (Fee Applies.)
learn more >
CONSTITUTIONAL LAW
Starts January 12
Examine our nation’s founding charter and the seminal Supreme Court cases interpreting it. Using Supreme Court opinions as a guide, this course will focus on the Constitution’s basic structure and design, investigating the principles of judicial review, federalism, and separation of powers. (Fee Applies.)
learn more >
THE SCIENCE OF WILLPOWER AND CHANGE
Starts February 9
This course explains what willpower is, why it matters, and how to develop it by drawing on psychology, neuroscience, medicine, and mind-body research. Addressing physical health, emotional well-being, social relationships, and professional success, this course will help you gain greater focus and motivation.(Fee Applies.)
learn more >
OTHER LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES

 
STANFORD INNOVATION AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP CERTIFICATE
Now Open!
Learn essential skills and effective strategies for working in and managing innovative organizations – and for starting new ones. Certificate requires 8 out of 12 self-paced courses and may be taken in any order. (Application and Fee Apply.)
learn more >
STANFORD LEAD CERTIFICATE: CORPORATE INNOVATION
Starts May 5, Application Deadline March 25
Master the key business fundamentals of finance, strategy, and critical thinking. Deepen your knowledge of methods for innovating through design thinking, building business models, and overcoming resistance to change. (Application and Fee Apply.)
learn more >
 
 

 
 
View our Catalog >
 
Thank you for your interest in learning opportunities available through Stanford Online. We offer many free, online courses taught by Stanford faculty to lifelong learners worldwide for self-enrichment. We also offer a variety of professional education opportunities in conjunction with many of the university's schools and departments. Courses available through Stanford Online are listed at http://online.stanford.edu/courses/.
 
Additional courses and programs are offered by: Stanford Center for Professional Development (School of Engineering), Stanford Innovation and Entrepreneurship (Graduate School of Business and School of Engineering), Continuing Medical Education (School of Medicine) and Continuing Studies. (Fees may apply.)

subject: Stanford Online Learning Opportunities
body:
[1]Stanford University

[2]Stanford Online

LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES

______________________________________________________________________

Upcoming Courses
______________________________________________________________________

[3]Cryptography I
[4]Cryptography I
Starts January 5
Protect information in computer systems by understanding the security of
cryptographic constructions and applying this knowledge in the real world.
(Free)
[5]Learn More >
[6]Game Theory II: Advanced Applications
[7]Game Theory II: Advanced Application
Starts January 11
Design interactions between agents to achieve good social outcomes through:
social choice theory (i.e. collective decision making), mechanism design,
and auctions. (Free)
[8]Learn More >
[9]International Women's Health and Human Rights
[10]International Women’s Health and Human Rights
Starts January 13
Take part in interactive discussions and cooperative exercises to learn how
to engage with your communities and deepen understanding of the issues
women face in real-life circumstances. (Free)
[11]Learn More >
[12]Statistical Learning
[13]Statistical Learning
Starts January 19
Focus on regression and classification methods, without heavy reliance on
mathematical formulas, through lectures devoted to R and tutorials that
progress with more detailed techniques. (Free)
[14]Learn More >
[15]Technology Entrepreneurship
[16]Technology Entrepreneurship
Starts January 5
Learn the processes used to develop startups, and work in teams to gain
knowledge and experience you can use to create a business model, market an
entrepreneurial idea to potential investors, and manage rapid growth.
(Free)
[17]Learn More >
______________________________________________________________________

Self-Paced Courses
______________________________________________________________________

[18]Child Nutrition and Cooking
[19]Child Nutrition and Cooking
Now Open
Examine what constitutes a healthy diet for children and adults, and learn
how to prepare simple, delicious foods aimed at inspiring a lifelong
celebration of easy home-cooked meals. (Free)
[20]Learn More >
[21]Computer Science 101
[22]Compilers
Now Open
Learn how a program written in a high-level language designed for humans is
systematically translated into a program written in low-level assembly more
suited to machines. This course will show you the interplay of theory and
practice in computer science, and how powerful general ideas combined with
engineering insight can lead to practical solutions to very hard problems.
(Free)
[23]Learn More >
[24]Databases
[25]Databases
Now Open
Take a set of mini-courses, which can be assembled in a variety of ways, to
learn about different aspects of databases like XML, JSON, SQL, and
Relational Design Theory. (Free)
[26]Learn More >
______________________________________________________________________

In Session Courses
______________________________________________________________________

[27]How to Learn Math for Students
[28]How to Learn Math for Students
Still Open
Get the information you need to become a powerful math learner. Grasp
fundamental concepts. Learn how to approach math effectively. Ideal for
basic and advanced levels of math. (Free)
[29]Learn More >

[30]How to Learn Math for Teachers and Parents
[31]How to Learn Math for Teachers and Parents
Still Open
Explore new research on mathematics learning and mindsets that can
transform your students' experiences with math. (Fee Applies)
[32]Learn More >
______________________________________________________________________

Continuing Medical Education
______________________________________________________________________

[33]How to Learn Math for Students
[34]Statistics for Medical Professionals (CME)
Now Open
Understand medical statistics as they pertain to practicing evidence-based
medicine, communicating treatment outcome probability, and interpreting the
results of studies and scientific papers to improve patient care quality,
(Fee Applies)
[35]Learn More >
______________________________________________________________________

Stanford Center for Professional Development
______________________________________________________________________

Graduate Courses for Academic Credit - Enroll in winter quarter courses by
December 8, 2014

[36]Biochemical Engineering
[37]Biochemical Engineering (CHEMENG150) (SCPD)
Starts January 5
Learn how to model social and economic networks and their impact on human
behavior. Contributes to the online Biotechnology Graduate Certificate.
(Application and Fee Apply)
[38]Learn More >
[39]Energy: Chemical Transformations for Production, Storage and Use
[40]Energy: Chemical Transformations for Production, Storage and Use
(CHEMENG25E) (SCPD)
Starts January 6
Discover the entrepreneurial opportunities and challenges of energy supply
and consumption by examining the composite of future large energy
production systems. Contributes to the online Molecular Engineering of
Energy Technologies Graduate Certificate. (Application and Fee Apply)
[41]Learn More >
______________________________________________________________________

Graduate School of Business Programs
______________________________________________________________________

[42]Stanford Lead Certificate
[43]Stanford LEAD Certificate: Corporate Innovation (GSB)
Starts May 5, 2015, Application Deadline March 25, 2015
Master the key business fundamentals of finance, strategy, and critical
thinking, then deepen your knowledge of methods for innovating through
design thinking, building business models, and overcoming resistance to
change. (Application and Fee Apply)
[44]Learn More >

[45]View our Catalog

Thank you for your interest in learning opportunities available through
[46]Stanford Online.
We offer many free, online courses taught by Stanford faculty to lifelong
learners worldwide for self-enrichment. We also offer a variety of
professional education opportunities in conjunction with many of the
university's schools and departments. Courses available through Stanford
Online are listed at [47]http://online.stanford.edu/courses/.

Additional courses and programs are offered by: [48]Stanford Center for
Professional Development (School of Engineering), [49]Stanford Innovation
and Entrepreneurship (Graduate School of Business and School of
Engineering), and [50]Continuing Medical Education (School of Medicine).
(Registration fees may apply.)

body_html:
LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES

 
Upcoming Courses

 
Cryptography I
Starts January 5
Protect information in computer systems by understanding the security of cryptographic constructions and applying this knowledge in the real world. (Free)
Learn More >
Game Theory II: Advanced Application
Starts January 11
Design interactions between agents to achieve good social outcomes through: social choice theory (i.e. collective decision making), mechanism design, and auctions. (Free)
Learn More >
International Women’s Health and Human Rights
Starts January 13
Take part in interactive discussions and cooperative exercises to learn how to engage with your communities and deepen understanding of the issues women face in real-life circumstances. (Free)
Learn More >
Statistical Learning
Starts January 19
Focus on regression and classification methods, without heavy reliance on mathematical formulas, through lectures devoted to R and tutorials that progress with more detailed techniques. (Free)
Learn More >
Technology Entrepreneurship
Starts January 5
Learn the processes used to develop startups, and work in teams to gain knowledge and experience you can use to create a business model, market an entrepreneurial idea to potential investors, and manage rapid growth. (Free)
Learn More >
Self-Paced Courses

 
Child Nutrition and Cooking
Now Open
Examine what constitutes a healthy diet for children and adults, and learn how to prepare simple, delicious foods aimed at inspiring a lifelong celebration of easy home-cooked meals. (Free)
Learn More >
Compilers
Now Open
Learn how a program written in a high-level language designed for humans is systematically translated into a program written in low-level assembly more suited to machines. This course will show you the interplay of theory and practice in computer science, and how powerful general ideas combined with engineering insight can lead to practical solutions to very hard problems. (Free)
Learn More >
Databases
Now Open
Take a set of mini-courses, which can be assembled in a variety of ways, to learn about different aspects of databases like XML, JSON, SQL, and Relational Design Theory. (Free)
Learn More >
In Session Courses
 
How to Learn Math for Students
Still Open
Get the information you need to become a powerful math learner. Grasp fundamental concepts. Learn how to approach math effectively. Ideal for basic and advanced levels of math. (Free)
Learn More >
How to Learn Math for Teachers and Parents
Still Open
Explore new research on mathematics learning and mindsets that can transform your students' experiences with math. (Fee Applies)
Learn More >
Continuing Medical Education
 
Statistics for Medical Professionals (CME)
Now Open
Understand medical statistics as they pertain to practicing evidence-based medicine, communicating treatment outcome probability, and interpreting the results of studies and scientific papers to improve patient care quality, (Fee Applies)
Learn More >
Stanford Center for Professional Development

Graduate Courses for Academic Credit - Enroll in winter quarter courses by December 8, 2014
 
Biochemical Engineering (CHEMENG150) (SCPD)
Starts January 5
Learn how to model social and economic networks and their impact on human behavior. Contributes to the online Biotechnology Graduate Certificate. (Application and Fee Apply)
Learn More >
Energy: Chemical Transformations for Production, Storage and Use    (CHEMENG25E) (SCPD)
Starts January 6
Discover the entrepreneurial opportunities and challenges of energy supply and consumption by examining the composite of future large energy production systems. Contributes to the online Molecular Engineering of Energy Technologies Graduate Certificate. (Application and Fee Apply)
Learn More >
Graduate School of Business Programs
 
Stanford LEAD Certificate: Corporate Innovation (GSB)
Starts May 5, 2015, Application Deadline March 25, 2015
Master the key business fundamentals of finance, strategy, and critical thinking, then deepen your knowledge of methods for innovating through design thinking, building business models, and overcoming resistance to change. (Application and Fee Apply)
Learn More >

 
View our Catalog
 
Thank you for your interest in learning opportunities available through Stanford Online.
We offer many free, online courses taught by Stanford faculty to lifelong learners worldwide for self-enrichment. We also offer a variety of professional education opportunities in conjunction with many of the university's schools and departments. Courses available through Stanford Online are listed at http://online.stanford.edu/courses/.
 
Additional courses and programs are offered by: Stanford Center for Professional Development (School of Engineering), Stanford Innovation and Entrepreneurship (Graduate School of Business and School of Engineering), and Continuing Medical Education (School of Medicine). (Registration fees may apply.)
